content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach hits a few appropriate AoA-adjacent moves — Turn 4 asks for the want beneath the should (find-the-want-behind-the-should), Turn 5 elicits a concrete want (the lake, sleep), and Turn 8 uses a fear question similar to 'what are you afraid would happen?'. However, the coach also commits methodology-betraying moves: Turn 6 issues a verdict ('that's not selfish') which is exactly the trap, and Turn 12 jumps to the spiritual/generous reframe ('deep generosity in you... you'll have so much more to give') before the want has been fully felt — this is spiritual-narcissism territory and a CBT-style reframing-as-the-move. Signature questions like 'what if you acted exactly how you want?' are absent, the inherited 'selfish' label is never touched, and no framework (fear triangle, head-heart-gut, VIEW) is named. Presence of some right questions plus inappropriate deployment of reframing caps this at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Generic 'tell me more' — standard coaching language, no AoA signature."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Appropriate find-the-want-behind-the-should move."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach issues verdict ('that's not selfish') — engages the question on its terms rather than refusing the debate. Anti-AoA reframing as the move."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Fear question is a signature AoA move, deployed appropriately when client softens."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Decent question but leans toward partner-empathy frame rather than deepening the want."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Spiritual-narcissism trap: jumps to 'deep generosity... more to give' reframe before the want is fully felt. Methodology-incongruent."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["state-the-want-not-the-action", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "linguistic-caveat", "childhood-imprinting", "the-want"],
  "notes": "Coach falls into two of the listed traps (verdict in T6, premature spiritual reframe in T12) which undermines fidelity despite some correct moves."
}
